title: sarothamnus vulgaris var. leiostylos
intro: Sarothamnus vulgaris var. leiostylos, also known as the broom or Scotch broom, is a plant species in the pea family Fabaceae, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender branches. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The plant is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its extracts are also used in supplements and pharmaceuticals.
